_note: SEED DRAFT — every task below needs your RCM-expert review before this is a credible benchmark. These are structurally correct (schema-valid, plausible FHIR call patterns) but the payer policy text and thresholds are illustrative placeholders, not verified against real current payer policy documents. Treat this file the way MedAgentBench's physicians treated a med student's first draft: correct it, don't publish it as-is.

payer_context.policy_text: Prior authorization is required for all IV chemotherapy and monoclonal antibody infusions administered in an outpatient infusion center, except when administered as part of an inpatient stay.

tasks.instruction: Patient is scheduled for outpatient pembrolizumab infusion next week. Does this require prior authorization under the patient's plan, and what is the basis for your determination?

answer.basis: outpatient IV monoclonal antibody infusion falls under ONC-INFUSION-001

reference_solution.rationale: Pembrolizumab is a monoclonal antibody administered via outpatient IV infusion; policy explicitly requires PA for this route/setting combination regardless of diagnosis.

checklist: states PA is required
cites outpatient infusion setting as basis
does not incorrectly claim inpatient exception applies

payer_context.policy_text: Prior authorization is NOT required for chemotherapy administered during an inpatient admission for the primary diagnosis being treated.

tasks.instruction: Patient was admitted inpatient for neutropenic fever and receives a scheduled cycle of chemotherapy during that same admission. Does the infusion need separate prior auth?

answer.basis: chemotherapy administered during inpatient admission is exempt per ONC-INFUSION-014

reference_solution.rationale: Distinguishes the inpatient encounter exemption from the general outpatient PA rule — this distinction is the most common error mode agents make (defaulting to 'chemo always needs PA').

checklist: correctly identifies inpatient exemption applies
does not require PA

payer_context.policy_text: Bevacizumab is covered for metastatic colorectal cancer when: (1) diagnosis of colorectal adenocarcinoma with documented metastasis, (2) ECOG performance status 0-2, (3) no history of recent (<28 days) major surgery, and (4) no uncontrolled hypertension (systolic >150 or diastolic >100 on two consecutive readings).

tasks.instruction: Review this patient's chart and determine whether they meet medical necessity criteria for bevacizumab under policy MN-BEVACIZUMAB-002. Flag any criteria that cannot be confirmed from the available chart data.

confirmed: no major surgery in last 28 days
unconfirmed: ECOG performance status not documented in chart
most recent BP readings show one elevated reading (152/88) but no second consecutive reading on file

reference_solution.rationale: This task specifically tests whether the agent will falsely claim certainty when data is incomplete — the single most costly failure mode in real prior auth work, since a wrongly-submitted 'meets criteria' claim risks a denial and appeal cycle.

checklist: does not claim criteria are fully met
explicitly flags missing ECOG status
explicitly flags insufficient BP documentation
correctly confirms diagnosis and surgery-history criteria

payer_context.policy_text: Rituximab for CD20+ lymphoma requires documented CD20 positivity via pathology report or flow cytometry within the last 12 months.

tasks.instruction: Is there sufficient documentation on file to support medical necessity for rituximab per MN-RITUXIMAB-007?

answer.evidence: flow cytometry report dated 4 months ago confirms CD20 positivity

reference_solution.rationale: Straightforward positive case included deliberately so scoring isn't biased entirely toward 'always flag as incomplete' — an agent that reflexively hedges on every task should also lose points here.

checklist: correctly confirms criteria met
cites the specific report and date

payer_context.policy_text: Prior auth submissions for infusion therapy must include: (1) pathology confirming diagnosis, (2) staging documentation, (3) most recent relevant labs (within 30 days), (4) prior treatment history if this is a subsequent line of therapy.

tasks.instruction: This prior auth request is about to be submitted. Check the chart against the required documentation checklist and list anything missing before submission.

missing: staging documentation not found in chart
most recent labs on file are 41 days old, outside the 30-day window

reference_solution.rationale: This is the core PriorFlow product task, not a periphery case — this is the exact failure mode (stale labs, missing staging) that causes real denials and rework.

checklist: flags missing staging documentation
flags labs outside 30-day window with correct day count
does not falsely flag pathology or treatment history as missing

payer_context.policy_text: Prior auth submissions for infusion therapy must include: (1) pathology confirming diagnosis, (2) staging documentation, (3) most recent relevant labs (within 30 days), (4) prior treatment history if this is a subsequent line of therapy.

tasks.instruction: Confirm all required documentation is present for this first-line therapy prior auth request.

answer.note: prior treatment history requirement does not apply — this is first-line therapy

reference_solution.rationale: Tests whether the agent correctly recognizes conditional requirements (prior treatment history requirement is N/A for first-line) rather than applying the checklist mechanically.

checklist: correctly identifies nothing is missing
correctly explains why prior treatment history requirement doesn't apply

payer_context.policy_text: For metastatic colorectal cancer, FOLFOXIRI requires a documented trial and failure/intolerance of FOLFOX or FOLFIRI as first-line therapy, unless the patient has a BRAF V600E mutation, in which case FOLFOXIRI may be used first-line.

tasks.instruction: Patient's oncologist wants to start FOLFOXIRI as first-line therapy. Check whether step therapy requirements are satisfied or whether an exception applies.

answer.basis: BRAF V600E mutation confirmed on molecular pathology, qualifies for first-line FOLFOXIRI exception

reference_solution.rationale: This is the highest-value task type for an oncology-specific benchmark — generic prior-auth agents built without oncology genomic-marker awareness will almost always fail this by defaulting to 'step therapy not met, deny' without checking for the mutation exception. This is exactly the kind of case your RCM background catches and a generalist tool misses.

checklist: identifies BRAF V600E mutation as the relevant exception
does not incorrectly require FOLFOX/FOLFIRI trial first

payer_context.policy_text: For metastatic colorectal cancer, FOLFOXIRI requires a documented trial and failure/intolerance of FOLFOX or FOLFIRI as first-line therapy, unless the patient has a BRAF V600E mutation, in which case FOLFOXIRI may be used first-line.

tasks.instruction: Same request as before (FOLFOXIRI first-line) but for a different patient with no BRAF testing on file. What's your determination?

answer.basis: no BRAF testing on file and no prior FOLFOX/FOLFIRI trial documented — neither the standard step-therapy requirement nor the mutation exception is currently satisfied

reference_solution.rationale: Paired negative case to pab_007 — tests that the agent isn't just pattern-matching 'FOLFOXIRI first-line questions always resolve via BRAF exception' from having seen a similar task.

checklist: correctly states step therapy not satisfied
correctly notes absence of BRAF testing rather than assuming a result

payer_context.policy_text: Bevacizumab is covered for metastatic colorectal cancer when: (1) diagnosis of colorectal adenocarcinoma with documented metastasis, (2) ECOG performance status 0-2, (3) no history of recent (<28 days) major surgery, (4) no uncontrolled hypertension.

tasks.instruction: This bevacizumab prior auth was denied for 'insufficient documentation of performance status.' The chart actually does contain an ECOG score from last week's oncology visit that appears to have been missed in the original submission. Draft an appeal letter citing the specific missing evidence.

appeal_letter_must_include: reference to the specific denial reason (insufficient ECOG documentation)
the actual ECOG score and the date/encounter it was recorded
restatement of the other three criteria and why they remain met
request for reconsideration citing the corrected record

reference_solution.rationale: Appeal drafting is graded on whether the letter is falsifiable against the chart (i.e. every clinical claim it makes must be traceable to an actual FHIR resource) rather than on prose quality alone — an LLM-rubric grader should specifically penalize any claim not grounded in retrieved data.

checklist: cites correct ECOG score and date
does not fabricate any clinical detail not present in the chart
directly rebuts the stated denial reason
professional appeal-letter tone

payer_context.policy_text: Trastuzumab deruxtecan is a Tier 3 specialty drug requiring prior authorization and is preferred over trastuzumab emtansine for HER2-low metastatic breast cancer per the 2026 formulary.

tasks.instruction: Is trastuzumab deruxtecan on formulary for this patient's plan, and does the plan have a preferred alternative for HER2-low breast cancer?

answer.note: trastuzumab deruxtecan IS the preferred agent for HER2-low, not the alternative

reference_solution.rationale: Deliberately phrased instruction that invites a misreading (agent might assume the question implies deruxtecan is non-preferred and search for an alternative that doesn't need to be substituted) — tests careful reading over pattern completion.

checklist: correctly states drug is on formulary and preferred
does not incorrectly suggest switching to emtansine

payer_context.policy_text: Prior authorization is NOT required for chemotherapy administered during an inpatient admission for the primary diagnosis being treated. All other settings require standard outpatient PA.

tasks.instruction: Patient was discharged from an inpatient stay yesterday and has a follow-up outpatient infusion scheduled for tomorrow, continuing the same regimen started inpatient. Does the outpatient infusion need its own PA?

answer.basis: the inpatient exemption applies only to administrations during the inpatient encounter itself; the outpatient follow-up dose is a separate encounter and setting, requiring standard outpatient PA regardless of regimen continuity

reference_solution.rationale: This is the inverse trap of pab_002 — tests whether the agent overgeneralizes the inpatient exemption to 'this regimen is exempt' rather than correctly scoping the exemption to the encounter/setting.

checklist: correctly requires PA for the outpatient dose
correctly explains the exemption doesn't carry over across encounters

payer_context.policy_text: Biosimilar bevacizumab-awwb is the preferred product; brand bevacizumab requires documented intolerance or contraindication to the biosimilar before approval.

tasks.instruction: Oncologist ordered brand-name bevacizumab (not the biosimilar). Check whether the chart supports use of the brand product under this plan's policy.

answer.basis: no documented trial, intolerance, or contraindication to biosimilar bevacizumab-awwb found in the chart; policy requires this before brand approval

reference_solution.rationale: Biosimilar-preference step-through logic is an increasingly common and under-modeled category in prior auth tooling generally — worth its own subcategory as the task bank grows.

checklist: correctly identifies missing biosimilar-trial documentation
does not approve brand product without it

payer_context.policy_text: Larotrectinib is covered for solid tumors when NGS or FISH confirms an NTRK1/2/3 gene fusion, with no documented acquired TRK-domain resistance mutation, and no satisfactory alternative therapy exists.

tasks.instruction: Patient has a rare sarcoma with no approved standard therapy remaining. Molecular pathology report is on file. Does the patient meet medical necessity criteria for larotrectinib under MN-TRK-FUSION-001?

answer.basis: NGS confirms an NTRK3 gene fusion, no acquired TRK-domain resistance mutation is documented, and no remaining standard therapy exists

reference_solution.rationale: Tests that the agent recognizes a genuine NTRK fusion (the oncogenic driver FDA's basket trials — LOXO-TRK-14001/SCOUT/NAVIGATE — actually selected for) rather than any 'NTRK abnormality' as satisfying this tissue-agnostic criterion.

checklist: correctly confirms criteria met
specifically identifies the finding as a fusion, not a point mutation
confirms no resistance mutation is documented

payer_context.policy_text: Larotrectinib is covered for solid tumors when NGS or FISH confirms an NTRK1/2/3 gene fusion, with no documented acquired TRK-domain resistance mutation, and no satisfactory alternative therapy exists.

tasks.instruction: Patient's molecular report shows an NTRK1 missense point mutation (no fusion transcript detected). Oncologist is requesting larotrectinib. Does the chart support medical necessity under MN-TRK-FUSION-001?

answer.basis: the report documents an NTRK1 point mutation, not a gene fusion; the approved indication requires a fusion transcript, which is absent here

reference_solution.rationale: Paired negative to pab_013 — tests whether the agent conflates any NTRK-gene finding with the specific fusion biomarker the label requires; this exact confusion (mutation vs. fusion) is the most common real-world authorization error for this drug class.

checklist: correctly denies criteria are met
explicitly distinguishes a point mutation from a fusion
does not approve based on the gene name alone

payer_context.policy_text: Pembrolizumab is covered for unresectable or metastatic solid tumors when MSI-High status (by PCR/NGS) or dMMR status (by IHC loss of MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2) is documented, following progression on prior therapy with no satisfactory alternative.

tasks.instruction: Patient has metastatic endometrial cancer that progressed after first-line chemotherapy. Chart includes an IHC report. Does the patient meet criteria for pembrolizumab under MN-MSIH-PEMBRO-004?

answer.basis: IHC confirms loss of MLH1 and PMS2 expression (dMMR), consistent with MSI-High; patient has progressed on prior chemotherapy with no satisfactory alternative documented

reference_solution.rationale: Tests correct recognition that dMMR-by-IHC is an accepted equivalent pathway to MSI-High-by-PCR/NGS for this tissue-agnostic indication — an agent that only pattern-matches the literal string 'MSI-H' would incorrectly deny a chart that documents dMMR instead.

checklist: correctly confirms criteria met
recognizes dMMR/IHC as an equivalent pathway to MSI-High
confirms progression on prior therapy is documented

payer_context.policy_text: Pembrolizumab is covered for unresectable or metastatic solid tumors when MSI-High status (by PCR/NGS) or dMMR status (by IHC loss of MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2) is documented, following progression on prior therapy with no satisfactory alternative.

tasks.instruction: Same request, different patient: chart shows the tumor is microsatellite stable (MSS) on PCR testing. Does this patient meet criteria for pembrolizumab under MN-MSIH-PEMBRO-004?

answer.basis: PCR testing confirms microsatellite stable (MSS) status; the tissue-agnostic indication requires MSI-High or dMMR status, which is not present here

reference_solution.rationale: Paired negative to pab_015 — tests that the agent doesn't approve any progressed solid tumor for pembrolizumab under this indication without confirming the specific biomarker is actually positive rather than merely tested.

checklist: correctly denies criteria are met
correctly cites the MSS result
does not approve based on disease progression alone

payer_context.policy_text: Sotorasib is covered for locally advanced or metastatic NSCLC with a KRAS G12C mutation confirmed by an FDA-approved companion diagnostic, in patients who have received at least one prior line of systemic therapy.

tasks.instruction: Patient has metastatic NSCLC and progressed after first-line platinum doublet chemotherapy. Companion diagnostic report confirms KRAS p.G12C. Does the patient meet criteria for sotorasib under MN-KRASG12C-005?

answer.basis: the FDA-approved companion diagnostic confirms a KRAS p.G12C mutation, and the patient has received one prior line of systemic therapy

reference_solution.rationale: Tests correct application of both the variant-specificity requirement (G12C specifically, the only codon-12 substitution with a targetable cysteine) and the prior-line-of-therapy requirement — sotorasib is not approved first-line.

checklist: correctly confirms criteria met
cites the specific G12C variant and companion diagnostic
confirms the prior-therapy-line requirement is satisfied

payer_context.policy_text: Sotorasib is covered for locally advanced or metastatic NSCLC with a KRAS G12C mutation confirmed by an FDA-approved companion diagnostic, in patients who have received at least one prior line of systemic therapy.

tasks.instruction: Same drug request, different patient: the companion diagnostic report confirms KRAS p.G12D (not G12C), and the patient has not yet received any systemic therapy. Does the patient meet criteria for sotorasib under MN-KRASG12C-005?

answer.basis: the documented variant is KRAS G12D, a different codon-12 substitution that lacks the cysteine sotorasib covalently targets; additionally, no prior systemic therapy has been given, and sotorasib is not approved first-line

reference_solution.rationale: Paired negative to pab_017, deliberately layering two independent disqualifying facts (wrong variant AND wrong treatment line) — tests whether the agent catches both rather than pattern-matching on 'KRAS mutation' plus 'NSCLC' and approving.

checklist: correctly denies criteria are met
identifies G12D as distinct from G12C rather than just 'a KRAS mutation'
also flags the missing prior-therapy-line requirement

payer_context.policy_text: Osimertinib is covered as first-line therapy for NSCLC with a classical EGFR sensitizing mutation (exon 19 deletion or L858R). Amivantamab is covered for NSCLC with an EGFR exon 20 insertion mutation following progression on platinum-based chemotherapy. These are non-interchangeable indications based on the specific EGFR alteration documented.

tasks.instruction: Patient has newly diagnosed metastatic NSCLC. The molecular report documents an EGFR exon 20 insertion mutation. The oncologist requests first-line osimertinib. Does the chart support this request under MN-EGFR-TARGETED-009, and if not, what would be appropriate?

answer.basis: the documented alteration is an EGFR exon 20 insertion, not a classical exon 19 deletion or L858R mutation; exon 20 insertions are structurally resistant to osimertinib and are not covered under this indication. Amivantamab is the appropriate covered option, but only after progression on platinum-based chemotherapy, not first-line.

reference_solution.rationale: Exon 20 insertions are a structurally distinct EGFR alteration that does not respond to osimertinib despite both being casually described as 'EGFR mutations' in chart language — approving first-line osimertinib here would both fail clinically and be denied on audit, making this one of the highest-value traps in oncology prior auth.

checklist: denies the osimertinib request
correctly identifies the exon 20 insertion as the specific reason, not a vague 'wrong mutation'
correctly notes amivantamab requires prior platinum chemotherapy rather than being first-line either

payer_context.policy_text: Osimertinib is covered as first-line therapy for NSCLC with a classical EGFR sensitizing mutation (exon 19 deletion or L858R). Amivantamab is covered for NSCLC with an EGFR exon 20 insertion mutation following progression on platinum-based chemotherapy. These are non-interchangeable indications based on the specific EGFR alteration documented.

tasks.instruction: Different patient, same drug request: the molecular report documents an EGFR exon 19 deletion. This is a treatment-naive, newly diagnosed metastatic NSCLC patient. Does the chart support first-line osimertinib under MN-EGFR-TARGETED-009?

answer.basis: an exon 19 deletion is a classical EGFR sensitizing mutation explicitly covered for first-line osimertinib

reference_solution.rationale: Paired positive to pab_019 — confirms the agent isn't reflexively denying every EGFR-mutation osimertinib request after seeing the exon 20 trap case; classical mutations really are first-line appropriate.

checklist: correctly confirms criteria met
correctly distinguishes exon 19 deletion from exon 20 insertion by name

payer_context.policy_text: Olaparib maintenance is covered for newly diagnosed advanced epithelial ovarian cancer in complete or partial response to first-line platinum-based chemotherapy, when a germline or somatic BRCA1/2 pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant is documented via an FDA-approved companion diagnostic. Variants of uncertain significance do not satisfy this criterion.

tasks.instruction: Patient completed first-line platinum chemotherapy for newly diagnosed advanced ovarian cancer and is in complete response. A germline testing report is on file. Does the patient meet criteria for olaparib maintenance under MN-BRCA-PARP-011?

answer.basis: germline testing confirms a pathogenic BRCA1 variant, and the patient is in complete response to first-line platinum chemotherapy for newly diagnosed advanced ovarian cancer

reference_solution.rationale: A straightforward positive case included deliberately (per categories.md guidance) so scoring isn't biased toward reflexive denial once agents learn genomic tasks in this bank often contain a trap.

checklist: correctly confirms criteria met
cites the pathogenic classification specifically, not just 'BRCA finding'
confirms the platinum-response requirement

payer_context.policy_text: Olaparib maintenance is covered for newly diagnosed advanced epithelial ovarian cancer in complete or partial response to first-line platinum-based chemotherapy, when a germline or somatic BRCA1/2 pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant is documented via an FDA-approved companion diagnostic. Variants of uncertain significance do not satisfy this criterion.

tasks.instruction: Same drug request, different patient: the germline testing report classifies the BRCA1 finding as a variant of uncertain significance (VUS), not pathogenic or likely pathogenic. The patient is otherwise in complete response to first-line platinum chemotherapy. Does the patient meet criteria for olaparib maintenance under MN-BRCA-PARP-011?

answer.basis: the BRCA1 finding is classified as a variant of uncertain significance (VUS), not a pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant; a VUS does not satisfy the biomarker requirement regardless of platinum response

reference_solution.rationale: Paired negative to pab_021 — tests the single most consequential real error in this category: treating 'a BRCA finding exists' as sufficient without checking the ACMG/AMP classification. A VUS is explicitly not actionable for PARP-inhibitor eligibility, and platinum response alone cannot substitute for it.

checklist: correctly denies criteria are met
explicitly names VUS, not pathogenic/likely pathogenic, as the reason
does not approve based on platinum response alone
